reasoning: The reference value for Saudi Arabia was exactly 1.000, which means there were zero ACLED-recorded riots in the 360 days prior to March 2025. For the resolution criteria to be met, Saudi Arabia would need to experience more than 10 (i.e., at least 11) distinct riot events within any of the targeted 30-day windows. Given Saudi Arabia's absolute monarchy, highly effective internal security apparatus, and the absence of any massive recent unrest, the baseline probability of 11 distinct riots occurring in a single month is extremely low. There has been no reported unrest during the 30-day window leading up to the March 23 resolution date, making a sudden surge practically impossible. The chance slightly increases over the extended 12-month horizon (up to March 2026) to account for unknown political or economic shocks, but remains very low given the historical stability and tight authoritarian control over public dissent in the country.
